# Easy.Platform - Copilot Instructions

> A .NET 9 framework for building microservices with CQRS, event-driven architecture, and multi-database support. Includes PlatformExampleApp (TextSnippet) as reference implementation.

<!-- CK:WORKFLOW-GATE -->

> **[WORKFLOW-GATE] — routing is your FIRST action, before any tool call.**
> This rule is hook-independent: it binds Claude, Codex, and Copilot equally. Do not wait for any injected reminder to apply it.
>
> Classify complexity and risk first, then route it:
>
> | Request is about…                                                  | Default route                                                                                                                                       |
> | ------------------------------------------------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
> | A simple, straightforward task with a clear target and low risk    | **direct execution** — do it without a workflow                                                                                                     |
> | A simple task that needs a few coordinated steps or skills         | **custom simple workflow** — sequence only the necessary skills/steps                                                                               |
> | A non-trivial bug, error, crash, regression, or wrong/stale output | **`workflow-bugfix` workflow** — `/start-workflow workflow-bugfix`                                                                                  |
> | A non-trivial new feature, capability, or enhancement              | **`workflow-feature` workflow** — `/start-workflow workflow-feature` (use `workflow-big-feature` when scope is large, ambiguous, or research-heavy) |
> | Anything matching a skill's or workflow's "Use" clause             | that skill / workflow                                                                                                                               |
> | A one-off question, or a truly trivial edit                        | direct execution                                                                                                                                    |
>
> 1. **An explicit `/skill` or `/workflow` in the prompt is the user's choice — execute it directly.** Otherwise auto-select the route yourself; never ask the user which path to take.
> 2. **Analyze whether the task is simple and straightforward before defaulting to a standard workflow.** If the target is clear, the change is low-risk, and a short direct execution can satisfy it, choose direct execution.
> 3. **For simple but multi-step work, build a custom simple workflow with only the few relevant skills/steps.** Do not expand to a full standard workflow when a small custom sequence is enough.
> 4. **Use standard workflows for non-trivial bugs and feature/enhancement work** — they force the investigation, tests, and review that risky or broad changes need.
> 5. **Declare the route, then ACTIVATE it — declaring is not activating.** State `Route: {workflow-id | skill | custom-simple | direct} — because {reason}`, then:
>     - **Workflow route →** invoke `/start-workflow <id>` as a tool call. That skill loads the workflow's canonical step `sequence` and creates the task list **1:1** from it. You MUST NOT hand-author your own task list for a workflow route — the canonical `sequence` is the only source of truth. Writing `Route: …` in prose and then improvising a few tasks is the failure this gate exists to prevent.
>     - **Skill route →** invoke that skill via the `Skill` tool.
>     - **Custom simple workflow →** create a small task list from the selected skills/steps, then execute them in order.
>     - **Direct route →** build the task list yourself, then proceed.
>       In every case the route must be activated BEFORE the first edit, sub-agent, or command.
> 6. **Direct execution is a legitimate route** for trivial, one-off, or simple straightforward work — but the declare-route and activate steps still apply.

<!-- /CK:WORKFLOW-GATE -->

---

## TL;DR - Golden Rules

1. **Repositories** — Use `IPlatformRootRepository<TEntity>` or service-specific repository interfaces
2. **Validation** — `PlatformValidationResult` fluent API (`.And()`, `.AndAsync()`), NEVER throw exceptions
3. **Side Effects** — Entity Event Handlers in `UseCaseEvents/`, NEVER in command handlers
4. **DTO Mapping** — DTOs own mapping via `PlatformEntityDto<TEntity, TKey>.MapToEntity()` or `PlatformDto<T>.MapToObject()`, NEVER map in handlers
5. **Cross-Service** — RabbitMQ message bus ONLY, NEVER direct database access
6. **Frontend State** — `PlatformVmStore` + `effectSimple()`, NEVER manual signals or direct `HttpClient`
7. **Base Classes** — Always extend `AppBaseComponent`/`AppBaseVmStoreComponent`/`AppBaseFormComponent` + `.pipe(this.untilDestroyed())` + BEM classes on all template elements. Extend `PlatformApiService` for HTTP calls.

**Architecture Hierarchy** - Place logic in LOWEST layer: `Entity/Model > Service > Component/Handler`

**Decision Quick-Ref:**

| Task               | Pattern                                                        |
| ------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------- |
| New API endpoint   | `PlatformBaseController` + CQRS Command                        |
| Business logic     | Command Handler (Application layer)                            |
| Data access        | `IPlatformRootRepository<TEntity>` + extensions                |
| Cross-service sync | Entity Event Consumer (message bus)                            |
| Scheduled task     | `PlatformApplicationBackgroundJob`                             |
| Migration          | `PlatformDataMigrationExecutor` / EF migrations                |
| Simple component   | Extend `AppBaseComponent`                                      |
| Complex state      | `AppBaseVmStoreComponent` + `PlatformVmStore`                  |
| Forms              | `AppBaseFormComponent` with validation                         |
| API calls          | Service extending `PlatformApiService`                         |
| Complex queries    | `RepositoryExtensions` with static expressions                 |
| Integration test   | Extend `PlatformServiceIntegrationTestWithAssertions<TModule>` |

---

## Search Existing Code FIRST

Before writing ANY code:

1. **Grep/Glob search** for similar patterns (find 3+ examples)
2. **Follow codebase pattern**, NOT generic framework docs
3. **Provide evidence** in plan (file:line references)

---

## Key File Locations

```
src/Platform/Easy.Platform/                             # Core framework library — CQRS, entities, validation, repositories, message bus abstractions
src/Platform/Easy.Platform.AspNetCore/                  # ASP.NET Core integration — controllers, middleware, DI
src/Platform/Easy.Platform.MongoDB/                     # MongoDB persistence provider
src/Platform/Easy.Platform.EfCore/                      # EF Core persistence provider (PostgreSQL, SQL Server)
src/Platform/Easy.Platform.RabbitMQ/                    # RabbitMQ message bus implementation
src/Backend/PlatformExampleApp.TextSnippet.Api/         # Example app API host — controllers, DI modules, startup
src/Backend/PlatformExampleApp.TextSnippet.Application/ # Example app application layer — CQRS commands, queries, DTOs, event handlers
src/Backend/PlatformExampleApp.TextSnippet.Domain/      # Example app domain layer — entities, repositories, domain events
src/Backend/PlatformExampleApp.IntegrationTests/        # Integration test suite for example app
src/Frontend/apps/playground-text-snippet/              # Angular frontend app for TextSnippet example
src/Frontend/libs/platform-core/                        # Frontend framework core — base components, stores, API services, utilities
```

---

## Project Reference Docs Index

> These docs contain detailed patterns and conventions. **READ the full file** when working in the matching context.
> Summaries below are concise — always read the source doc for complete guidance.

| Doc                                                                                  | Summary                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | READ When                                                                                                                                                                            |
| ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| [Backend Patterns Reference](docs/project-reference/backend-patterns-reference.md)   | Canonical Easy.Platform backend conventions with `file:line` evidence — entities (`RootAuditedEntity`, `[ComputedEntityProperty]`, static `Expression` filters), repository pattern, CQRS command/query handlers, `PlatformValidationResult` fluent validation (never throw), DTO-owned mapping, entity event handlers for side effects (`UseCaseEvents/`), RabbitMQ message bus, and background jobs.                                                      | Writing or modifying any backend code — CQRS commands/queries, entities, repositories, validation, DTOs, event handlers, message bus, migrations, or background jobs.                |
| [Domain Entities Reference](docs/project-reference/domain-entities-reference.md)     | Evidence-cited map of the TextSnippet domain model — entity base-class hierarchy (`RootEntity`/`RootAuditedEntity`/`IRowVersionEntity`), entity catalog, relationships, aggregate boundaries, DTO mapping ownership (`PlatformEntityDto.MapToEntity()`), value objects (`PlatformValueObject<T>.Validate()`), cross-service entity map via message bus, persistence, and frontend data models.                                                              | Designing or changing domain entities, value objects, aggregates, DTO mapping, or entity relationships; verifying correct base classes and mapping ownership before generating code. |
| [Frontend Patterns Reference](docs/project-reference/frontend-patterns-reference.md) | Angular 19 + Nx + NgRx ComponentStore + Material 3 conventions — `AppBase*` component base classes (never extend `Platform*` directly), `PlatformVmStore` state with `effectSimple()`/`tapResponse()`, view-model pattern, `PlatformApiService` HTTP services, DTO patterns, reactive forms, `.pipe(this.untilDestroyed())` subscription cleanup, and BEM on every template element.                                                                        | Creating or modifying Angular components, stores, view models, API services, forms, or templates; ensuring leak-free subscriptions and correct base-class usage.                     |
| [SCSS Styling Guide](docs/project-reference/scss-styling-guide.md)                   | BEM + token-driven SCSS rules — `block__element--modifier` naming (with the project's standalone `--modifier` class variant bound via `[class.--modifier]`), two-tier platform-core/`$app-*` tokens, `@use`/`@forward` (never `@import`), `flex-layout-media()` responsive mixin (never raw `@media`), mixins/functions reference, color palette, and theming. NEVER hardcode colors, pixels, font sizes, or z-index.                                       | Writing or editing any `.scss`/`.css` — applying BEM classes, using design tokens, responsive layout, or theming.                                                                    |
| [Integration Test Reference](docs/project-reference/integration-test-reference.md)   | Two integration-test styles — DEFAULT subcutaneous CQRS through real DI (no HTTP) extending `TextSnippetIntegrationTestBase`, and HTTP-level via `WebApplicationFactory<Program>`. xUnit + FluentAssertions; ALL tests use a `[Collection]` attribute and `IntegrationTestHelper.UniqueName()` for collision-free data; assert real DB state via polling; live infra with no DB reset. Covers base classes, fixtures/factories, helpers, and running tests. | Writing, reviewing, or fixing backend integration tests; choosing the correct base class/style; ensuring isolation, unique test data, and real-state assertions.                     |
| [E2E Test Reference](docs/project-reference/e2e-test-reference.md)                   | Resilient Playwright E2E for the `playground-text-snippet` Angular app — Page Object Model on `BasePage`, Angular-aware waits (never `networkidle`), API-level arrange/cleanup via `ApiHelpers` in `afterEach`, `createTestTask()`/`createTestSnippet()` factories, priority tags (`@P0`–`@P3`), import `test`/`expect` from `utils/test-helpers`, and selectors only in page objects.                                                                      | Authoring or maintaining Playwright E2E tests, page objects, fixtures, or wait/assertion patterns for the frontend app.                                                              |
| [Project Structure Reference](docs/project-reference/project-structure-reference.md) | Authoritative map of services, ports, directory layout, tech-stack versions, and module codes (all `file:line`-cited) — reusable framework set (`src/Platform/`) + layered example app (Api/Application/Domain/Infrastructure/Persistence) + Nx Angular frontend, orchestrated by Docker Compose. Covers service architecture, infra ports, CI/CD, and quick-start commands.                                                                                | Placing code in the correct project, looking up service ports/paths/module codes, tech-stack versions, or local startup commands.                                                    |
| [Code Review Rules](docs/project-reference/code-review-rules.md)                     | Repo-specific DO/DON'T review rules with real `file:line` anti-patterns — validation via `PlatformValidationResult` (never throw), side effects in entity event handlers, logic in lowest layer, DTO-owned mapping, RabbitMQ for cross-service, `AppBase*` frontend base classes. Includes backend/frontend/architecture rule sets, anti-patterns, decision trees, and checklists.                                                                          | Reviewing a PR or self-checking changes before commit; verifying repo-specific conventions instead of generic style advice.                                                          |
| [Feature Docs Reference](docs/project-reference/feature-docs-reference.md)           | Conventions for business feature docs — 26-section template, `file:line` evidence for every test case, app-to-service mapping (`playground-text-snippet` → TextSnippet layers + `text-snippet-domain`), backend layer paths, feature-doc structure, templates, and coverage gaps. Docs live under `docs/business-features/{Module}/detailed-features/`.                                                                                                     | Creating or updating business feature documentation, or locating the backend/frontend layers and doc directory for a feature.                                                        |
| [Documentation Index Reference](docs/project-reference/docs-index-reference.md)      | Navigation map for the Easy.Platform docs (glob-verified counts) — documentation system overview, doc graph, key doc relationships, and a Doc Lookup Guide mapping tasks to the right doc. `docs/project-reference/` holds canonical AI-context docs; `docs/templates/` holds authoring templates.                                                                                                                                                          | Finding which doc to open for a given task, or understanding the overall documentation structure and relationships.                                                                  |

---

## Development Commands

```bash
# Backend tests
dotnet test src/Backend/PlatformExampleApp.Test/              # backend-unit
dotnet test src/Backend/PlatformExampleApp.IntegrationTests/  # backend-integration
dotnet test src/Backend/PlatformExampleApp.Test.BDD/          # backend-bdd

# Frontend & E2E tests
cd src/Frontend && npm test                   # frontend-unit
cd src/Frontend/e2e && npx playwright test    # e2e

# Local startup (Docker)
src/start-dev-platform-example-app.cmd                # full system (default MongoDB+SQL)
src/start-dev-platform-example-app.infrastructure.cmd # infra only
```
